tests_written: health returns HTTP 200 with degraded database status when DATABASE_URL is absent
app router builds without a database pool
users, OAuth accounts, and sessions round-trip through Postgres migrations
session revocation removes sessions from active-session lookup
needs_deeper_qa: Run make db-migrate once sqlx-cli is installed or CI has a migration runner
Confirm the future tower-sessions store table contract is compatible with the sessions schema before wiring auth-001
Exercise /health against the provisioned RDS DATABASE_URL after preflight fills .env

tests_written: user-owned repositories enforce per-owner case-insensitive uniqueness
repository creation grants owner permissions and paginates through the standard list envelope
users cannot create repositories under a different user owner
organization repositories require org owner/admin membership
teams and team memberships persist under organizations
commit metadata and repository git refs round-trip through Postgres
needs_deeper_qa: make db-migrate still needs a sqlx-cli-equipped environment; the migration harness passed through cargo tests
Repository JSON routes are compiled but should be exercised through HTTP once auth/session middleware exists
Owner login for user-owned repositories currently falls back to email until username/profile setup ships

tests_written: default issue labels are created idempotently per repository
issues can be created, listed by status, commented on, reacted to, closed, and read through ordered timeline events
users without repository permissions cannot create issues
pull requests share the repository issue number sequence and keep a linked issue record
pull requests can be listed, fetched by number, merged, and read through ordered timeline events
make check, make test, and TEST_DATABASE_URL-backed make test passed
needs_deeper_qa: HTTP issue and pull request JSON routes are compiled but should be exercised through real auth once auth-001 lands
Concurrent issue and pull request creation should be stress-tested because number allocation currently uses max(number)+1
Milestone, cross-reference, and label editing APIs are schema-ready but not exposed until their product features are selected
Browser smoke test skipped because infra-001 phase 3 is backend schema/data access only

tests_written: Actions workflows, workflow runs, jobs, and steps round-trip through Postgres
Workflow run lifecycle transitions record started/completed timestamps and conclusions
Package records and package versions persist with case-insensitive per-package version uniqueness
Webhook registration and webhook delivery retry metadata persist, including response status and next retry time
Notifications can be created, listed, counted as unread, and marked read
Postgres-backed job leases enforce single-worker acquisition, failure retry, and completion
needs_deeper_qa: Actual Actions worker execution, logs, artifacts, and S3 storage are metadata-ready but not implemented in this phase
Webhook HTTP delivery signing and receiver calls need full QA when the delivery worker ships
Notification fanout and email delivery are schema-ready but not connected to SES yet
make db-migrate still needs sqlx-cli installed; cargo tests applied the migration through SQLx's migrator
Browser smoke test skipped because infra-001 phase 4 is backend schema/data access only

tests_written: search documents persist with full-text/trigram indexes and permission-aware query filtering
repository owners can index private search documents while unauthorized users cannot
public search documents remain visible to users without repository permissions
request logs persist method, path, status, duration, user agent, and selected non-secret metadata
personal access token prefixes and hashes are stored uniquely for later PAT support
shared list envelope serializes pageSize and shared error envelope preserves stable error codes
representative query plan for request log path lookup compiles against Postgres
needs_deeper_qa: Middleware request logging is wired into the Axum app but should be exercised over HTTP once authenticated routes are available
Search ranking is Postgres-based and should be tuned with real repository/code content after git ingestion ships
Down migrations exist for phase 5 tables but the environment still lacks sqlx-cli for make db-migrate
Browser smoke test skipped because infra-001 phase 5 is backend schema/data access only

tests_written: missing Google OAuth config returns a safe auth_not_configured error
unsafe next URLs are normalized to /dashboard to prevent open redirects
Google authorization URLs include callback URI, code response type, profile/email/openid scopes, and signed state
signed OAuth state decodes and preserves the sanitized next URL
.scratch/auth-oauth-start-scenario.sh starts the real Axum API and verifies HTTP 302 to Google
needs_deeper_qa: Real Google callback, token exchange, and userinfo fetch are Phase 2 and were not implemented in this phase
Session cookie issuance is not present until Phase 2, so QA should not expect /api/auth/me yet
Browser smoke test skipped because Phase 1 is a backend OAuth redirect endpoint only

tests_written: Google callback persistence upserts users and OAuth accounts from a fake Google userinfo payload
signed __Host-session cookie issuance includes HttpOnly and SameSite=Lax attributes
/api/auth/me returns the authenticated user when a valid signed session cookie is present
logout revokes the active session, clears the cookie, and remains idempotent
OAuth callback errors and missing authorization codes redirect to /login?error=oauth_failed without leaking provider details
make check, make test, and TEST_DATABASE_URL-backed make test passed
needs_deeper_qa: Real Google token exchange and userinfo fetch still need browser-level QA with TEST_ACCOUNT_EMAIL once the login UI ships
Cookie Secure/__Host behavior should be rechecked in Phase 4 hardening because local dev currently permits insecure cookies
Browser smoke test skipped because auth-001 Phase 2 is backend callback/session API only

tests_written: Next.js /login renders a compact opengithub sign-in card with only Continue with Google
OAuth start URL generation preserves safe next paths and normalizes unsafe external URLs
Login page renders inline oauth_failed callback errors
Anonymous /dashboard redirects to /login?next=/dashboard
Ever browser smoke verified the Google button navigates to the Rust /api/auth/google/start flow and reaches Google OAuth
needs_deeper_qa: Complete real Google account selection and callback with TEST_ACCOUNT_EMAIL in Phase 4
Authenticated dashboard rendering was not browser-verified because it requires a real Google callback session
Playwright auth setup and stored auth state are intentionally deferred to auth-001 Phase 4
npm install reported transitive dependency audit findings from scaffold/test tooling; no production dependency audit remediation was done in this phase
